channel.title: SCP Foundation - new forum posts
channel.description: Posts in forums of the site "SCP Foundation" - Secure, Contain, Protect

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Noting that this is a duplicate thread of this:
http://www.scpwiki.com/forum/t-13972797/my-idea#post-4865163

AgentGat0r, per the forum guidelines:

Do not create a new thread for a concept already on the forum, unless the current thread has not been posted on in 14 days or longer.

This is to help prevent flooding of the forums, and because it would make it less difficult for you and reviewers to keep track of posted content if it were all in one thread.

If you need to modify your concept, please use the edit function rather than reposting, and instead of waiting and opting for a repost due to the thread getting buried, authors are encouraged to reach out to reviewers. Please consider referring to this thread for guidance on how to contact them: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: My Small Idea

item.title: Re: A Baby Toy
item.content:encoded:
This sounds like a really cool idea!


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: A Baby Toy

item.title: A Baby Toy
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlight: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: SCP-XXXX is a spherical baby toy that has multiple buttons on its surface that look like different animals. All of these buttons have been classified SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5, and it also has a spiraling roulette with a turtle as the arrow which is classified as SCP-XXXX-6. When SCP-XXXX is near the vicinity of a child under the age of 4 years SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-6 will become animated this is its active phase, this will not happen if there is any individual above 4 years present in the room ,nor will it happen if there are any video recorders present in the room though audio recorders doesn't stop the active phase from happening.During SCP-XXXX active phase SCP-XXXX-1 through SCP-XXXX-5 will proceed to play with the children(s) ,or play with each other though ,SCP-XXXX-6 will not engage instead warning, or scolding the other SCP-XXXX instances if they make acts that could harm the children(s).

Description of SCP-XXXX-1 :has the appearance of a red cat with a joyful smile.
Description of SCP-XXXX-2 :has the appearance of a brown bear in a laid down position.
Description of SCP-XXXX-3 :has the appearance of a purple bird with its wings being fully shown.
Description of SCP-XXXX-4 :has the appearance of a blue dog that is running happily.
Description of SCP-XXXX-5 :has the appearance of a white rabbit in the middle of jumping.
Description of SCP-XXXX-6 :has the appearance of a turtle laid down on their underside.

[[collapsible]]

Central Narrative:
The central narrative of this piece would take the form of 1 audio recording with the SCP-XXXX instances,and 1 interview with them.
1 3 year old named David coming close to his birthday thus meeting SCP-XXXX requirement was put inside a room with SCP-XXXX ,and a audio recorder was hidden on the side of a beanie bag.(note:He was not very interested in SCP-XXXX)

David:What's happening to that toy?

SCP-XXXX-2:Well hello there buddy ,want to come play with us?

SCP-XXXX-3:Yeah you can join!!!…as long as you aren't rowdy as willy wagtail over there.

SCP-XXXX-4:Mind repeating that Miss Mary Quack?

SCP-XXXX-3:uhhh i didn't say anything.

David:Am i not supposed to be here?

SCP-XXXX-4:Sorry don't mind us, so are you going to play or no?

David:Yeah!so what game are we playing?

Hook/Attention Grabber: It's a baby toy ,and it has animals that is friendly to children

Additional Notes: i wanted to show how the audio log will look like sorry.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: A Baby Toy

item.title: Re: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
Howdy fluffal,
Flattered by the PM. If you saw me in another thread you may have seen my disclaimer, but I am not an official reviewer. So:
A. I cannot greenlight unfortunately
B. Take my feedback with a grain of salt compared to that of real reviewers like cybersquid & Dr Sage Evergreen.

Nevertheless, lets dig in.

Click to dig in
Click to un-dig in

As you may have noticed, you have a lot of support from some veterans here; so congrats. And I agree: I like the idea, I like the narrative, but execution is important to not spoil the surprise. Now parroting the others doesn't do you much good, so here is my thoughts. A possible means of execution is to make the depressive aspects of the object less noticeable, as in during initial testing on the object the foundation only observed the euphoric effects on others. But after acquisition of the tape, and addenda is made and the description is expanded upon. This idea may be up a similar path as Vivarium's but figured i'd add my own clarification. I also like Evergreen's proposal as well. Either way, I think you have a great idea here. My only other question is what compels Emily to continue using the pom-poms. While yes, I doubt that most people would jump to the conclusion that their cheer gear is the cause of their misery, many (and I am of course generalizing here), but many individuals suffering depression express decreased motivation in the things they love. Would that not have happened to her? I'm not saying that there has to be an anomalous explanation for this, but keep this question in mind while further weaving your narrative.

Hopefully I could be some help; Best of luck,
VoxStulti


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Chearleader Pom Poms[Seeking Greenlight]

This would benefit from rewording many of the instances of "The (noun) start to (verb)." I tend to read SCPs out loud, and after the third or fourth time I was really wishing it would get changed up a little.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5271

item.title: Re: Semi-Veil breaking Parawatch SCP idea
item.content:encoded:
+ Responses
- Close

This motivation feels a lot more Serpents Hand-y than it does Parawatch-y; I don't think this is a huge concern but it might be worth thinking about.

Parawatch members have a wide variety of motivations for being members of the wiki. Some of those motivations happen to align with the Serpents Hand, attempting to speak openly about anomalies, even if they don't have any idea what's going on.

My core concern is that this still feels extremely focused on the Parawatch poster: I don't think this is necessarily a bad idea but it feels like everything is being driven by what they do and how they feel about the situation and it feels to me like this will be really hard for you to convey via the framing of a SCP article, where you can't like, directly get into their head or anything.

Perhaps I was a bit unclear what I meant by 'Excerpts from Parawatch forums' in the page layout section. While the article will start as a SCP article there will be a link at the bottom of the page, with a title like "View ongoing containment measures". This will lead to a page filled with many Parawatch forum threads. It's through these threads that the story will be told and they will allow incite into the motivations and feelings of the poster. There will also be, as stated, an interview.

My other concern is there's a feeling of this lacking stakes: like it's obviously bad if Parawatch has all this information, and the foundation trying to shut down parawatch isn't a bad response or anything; but it feels like there's not really a sense of 'if parawatch does x, y will happen; so we have to prevent x'. On the flip side, there's not really a sense of the Foundation's actions having consequences either: like, if the Foundation does shut this down, what happens?

I feel that you missed the purpose of the revision. I have now changed the concept from "Parawatch makes a mass-movement to expose an anomaly to the public" to "A standard Foundation cover-up focusing on Parawatch and their responses". It is no longer a high-stakes situation, nor-with long reaching consequences. The Foundatiion is no longer trying to 'shut down' Parawatch, rather they are interfering with a single anomaly on the wiki which is proving more difficult than usual. This article is now mainly focused on Parawatch responding to the situation along with the reactions of one truth-seeker in particular and their interactions with each other and the Foundation are the story. Their are no stakes because the Foundation was always going to succeed somehow, but Parawatch doesn't no that and just keeps trying.

Iunno, really what I'm getting at is there's a lot of this I kinda like but it feels like it's missing something to just, really get it to work and I'm not quite sure what it is. It might be just some emotional framing: like, who are we rooting for here? What point are you making about releasing information about all anomalies to the public?

I am trying to get the readers to root for the Underdog, in this case Parawatch and that particular poster, despite knowing that they won't succeed. I also want to invoke feelings of sympathy for someone who has discovered and is exited about an anomaly, but is being shut down by the Foundation. As for releasing anomalies to the public, that isn't really a major theme to the article other than part of the poster's motivation to not be stopped and the reason they decline a Foundation position. However, if it was a part I suppose the message would be that the public may want and accept knowledge about the anomalous, but the Foundation won't let that happen because they believe the public isn't ready for it.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Semi-Veil breaking Parawatch SCP idea

item.title: Pelicans we, an SCP Article.
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: yes

Page type: SCP article

Elevator pitch: This SCP is a Stuffed Pelican that causes any individual who touches it, or is too close to begin to hallucinate that every living being is a pelican, as well as hearing the song "Pelicans we" by Cosmo Sheldrake at high volumes to the point that it hurts the ears and causes headaches. Sometimes also causes blurry vision. This SCP is to be only handled by an individual wearing a sealed HAZMAT suit and is kept in a sealed glass container with a danger line on the floor marking the range of effect.

Central Narrative: This SCP is marked as Safe and/or Explained as it was discovered that it was manufactured in the 1960s and was held in possession of a prolific drug user who primarily used inhalable hallucinogens. The drugs and the anomalous energy caused by the drug use infused into the pelican causing it's effects. It has not been neutralized or destroyed, and research is going towards neutralization.

Hook/Attention Grabber: Not many "explained" SCPs are in effect currently (at least that I'm aware of, there are too many to be fully aware of all of them now) and it uses a historical period where drugs were common. There is still an anomalous nature that is common in SCP articles, so it works well with fitting in with the rest of them as well as being it's own thing.

Additional notes: The actual idea is inspired by the song itself, so I decided it should be included into the SCP itself. The song itself sounds like a drug trip, and is considered creepy by some of my friends, so it seemed like a good idea to state it as a hallucinogenic SCP. Then it made more sense to have a pelican involved as the actual object, and a stuffed animal seemed like a fair play. It's normally a rather insignificant and everyday object that is changed by it's anomalous properties. I'm also looking for critiques to make the proposal/other proposals in the future better, not just the greenlight to make it an article.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Pelicans we, an SCP Article Proposal

item.title: JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
item.content:encoded:
Howdy hey Charvel,

As per the site Criticism Policy, forum critique should be relatively in-depth. Please edit your post to give the author a clearer direction to follow and note how your suggestions will improve their material. If you are unsure about approaching a more in-depth critique, feel free to let an experienced reviewer respond first so your critique can build on theirs.

For more information on giving critique in the Ideas Critique forum, please look at this thread.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Idea - A living Zebra

item.title: JS Staff Post -- Forum Crit -- Info
item.content:encoded:
Howdy hey FBFermi,

As per the site Criticism Policy, forum critique should be relatively in-depth. Please edit your post to give the author a clearer direction to follow and note how your suggestions will improve their material. If you are unsure about approaching a more in-depth critique, feel free to let an experienced reviewer respond first so your critique can build on theirs.

For more information on giving critique in the Ideas Critique forum, please look at this thread.

Author,

I suggest reaching out to someone on the Butterfly Squad Roster to seek additional critique on your idea.

Thank you.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Idea - A living Zebra

item.title: Re: My Eternal Crusade
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought this was a boring concept I had seen before until I got to the part where it said he allowed one SCP escape and that he actually had more personal motives that he is not disclosing. Don't bury that, I think this could be a very interesting take if you focus on how he has a very good reason for what he does. But be careful, this could be just another Able if you don't play it right. You have to describe that he is driven by something clear enough to distinguish him for similar anomalies.

Also, Omega-7 may be done, but Alpha-9 is doing it again with the Ressurection canon, work on it a little more and maybe he'll find a home there. However, bear in mind, I'm not authorized for green lighting anything so you still need to make this good enough for someone whose opinion actually matters.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: My Eternal Crusade

item.title: Re: What are your unusual headcanons?
item.content:encoded:
The numbering of the SCPs is chosen by the O5/Administrator and has a special in-universe meaning. That's why some old SCP have larger numbers and some SCP that can't exist in some canon doesn't have two entries.


Forum category: SCP Universe / Foundation Universe
Forum thread: What are your unusual headcanons?

item.title: My First Idea
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch:

  • Humanoid entity with metallic skin
  • Reaction to people it encounters depends on their morality

Central Narrative: The SCP was once a normal human woman, possibly someone who worked for the Foundation, who was badly mutilated and killed in an accident that may or may not have involved another SCP. A few hours after her death, her body spontaneously reanimated and morphed into a large humanoid entity with skin that appeared to be made of metal. It was then taken into Foundation custody, where its abilities and personality became apparent during testing.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The SCP's gimmick is that it reacts differently to people it encounters depending on what it perceives their morality to be. Upon looking at a person it encounters, it can instantly gauge their morality, though the standards by which it defines good and evil aren't entirely understood. If it sees someone as not being evil, it will typically ignore them, but if it sees someone as evil, it experiences a dramatic increase in aggression, often attempting to kill them.

Additional Notes: If anyone has any feedback or suggestions as to how I could elaborate upon or improve the idea, I'd be glad to hear it. If the idea is too similar to an existing SCP, please let me know, since I might not be aware since there are so many SCPs.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: My First Idea

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Hi, this is my introduction as a hopeful writer of future SCPs and tales. I have been writing for a long time, and have been a follower of the SCP foundation for a long time, although I don't have an exact length of time. I'm not entirely sure what more information I should put into the intro, so here goes :)

Gruvint8


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

Looking at the image in the history, it reminds me of ywhach from bleach.

Also liked the concept of the article and upvoted


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-1209

No vote. However as a parent, I'm forced to ask; what became of the monkey?


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-1327

item.title: Re: Social Suicide: SCP Idea
item.content:encoded:
This is a lot like SCP-286 the Cap of Neglect which starts out simply making you less noticeable but the more you use it the less anybody even remembers you until after 10 or 15 hours of use nobody remembers you at all. It mentions how there were records of one agent and yet absolutely nobody can remember him beyond the name appearing in payroll.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Social Suicide: SCP Idea

Seriously though, really nice stuff.

item.title: In which no babies are eaten
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Grigori Karpin and Ralliston for their crit. Site-36 is basically community property, and the community ought to do more with it.

SCPs obliquely referenced:

SCP-089: Tophet by spikebrennan
SCP-475: Soap of a Pope by mlister and Tarage
SCP-4263: Gemini by Tanhony

Tales directly referenced:

Empire of Dirt (Part 3 by Djoric
GRANT REQUEST FOR UTILIZATION OF ELECTRO-THAUMIC COMPUTERS TO PERFORM EXORCISMS OF DEMONIC ENTITIES by GreenWolf


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: The Moloch Mentality

Could I suggest a possible crosslink with SCP-2020? Because I think I just burst a kidney. +1.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-1313

item.title: Re: SCP Idea - A living Zebra
item.content:encoded:
This should automatically get a greenlight from it not being in the horror genre alone.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Idea - A living Zebra

item.title: Fungal Colony
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: An alien fungus lands in an isolated town, infecting the surrounding plants as well as many of the mammals, birds, and a portion of the people turning the latter into violent cannibals. At first it seems like a standard zombie scenario but the fungus is in fact sentient and sapient and the violent ones were the result of an accident when they first arrived.

Central Narrative: The article begins by showing the SCP as a typical zombie pathogen then goes into some detail about their extraterrestrial origins before shifting to the Foundation discovering crude notes leading to the house of a serial killer with the implication that not all those infected are violent and those that are were the result of said killer being one of the first infected and his tendencies along with the fungi having never encountered intelligent life, creating a negative reaction. It then ends with their pleas to return to meteorite (which is actually an unconventional generation ship) so they can seek out a new home.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: A twist on the typical zombie outbreak along with some exploration about the unusual form alien life might take and how difficult it may be to communicate.

Additional Notes: May get slightly technical due to some real life parasite biology I'd like to integrate.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Fungal Colony

I have absolutely no idea what this was about but I adore it regardless.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-3633

item.title: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?
item.content:encoded:
I'm making a skip and it involves the Foundation Front company, Spicy Crust Pizza. I'm wondering if that company has an official email in-universe. If there is one, do you know what the name of it is?


Forum category: SCP Universe / Foundation Universe
Forum thread: Does Spicy Crust Pizza have an email address?

item.title: Re: SCP Idea - A living Zebra
item.content:encoded:
This is a cool idea, I enjoy improbable odds. But I found it very similar to an explained SCP entry. You can read it here: SCP-123-EX

Maybe mix it up some more, make the ability more extreme maybe?


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Idea - A living Zebra

echoing this sentiment. like come on, isn't this basically just that south park episode, but in SCP format?


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5607

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! I had a lot of fun with it. Less fun was trying to keep it from being long but keep the characterization going, but I'm happy it worked out in the end.

Brendamat OTP indeed.

Hope you like my other stuff!


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5866

item.title: Re: Clinical Deficency
item.content:encoded:
If you want to poke fun at clinical tone, you need a plausible connection as to how it is that way, because surprise, surprise - 'back in my days', clinical tone still existed, and using that reason is still very much non-sequitur-ish.

Furthermore, to make fun of something, you need to have a good grasp of it already, which I can't say one way or another you possess.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Clinical Deficency

item.title: Re: Clinical Deficency
item.content:encoded:
Ok, the origin of the SCP, I agree needs a lot of work, and I'm willing to change it.

this feels like an excuse to write an scp in casual and nonclinical tone

I don't think you understand, this is a joke scp. I'm trying to poke fun at the foundation that they care so much about their clinical tone. obviously, the foundation wouldn't spend a tenth of their budget because they want to sound proffesional.

If you still think I should scrap this, I'm ready to, but not with the current reasons you've given.

Thanks for the crit Nicolini


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Clinical Deficency

item.title: (SEEKING GREENLIGHT) an existential philosophy
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes. got crit in IRC

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Philosophical

Elevator Pitch: A philosophy that makes SCP personnel realize that they are story characters

Central Narrative: The SCP foundation will discover that they are not real and suppress this information from not only the world but also themselves

Hook/Attention-Grabber: I'm going to have not only that personnel realize they are story characters but also they will realize the reader is there

Additional Notes: any critique or feedback welcome, I know this is a complex idea but i'm sure that I can run away with the idea


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (SEEKING GREENLIGHT) an existential philosophy

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for being so fast in answering me! You've got really great points that I'll try to answer.

  1. About the ballet dancing, I will be an indicative of the SCP adaptations everytime it meets a new owner. It's necessary storywise as it shows that the sword can adapt every skill of its owner (even dancing) as a method of combat.
  2. About both the hosts and the Foundation, I was thinking about this. In my vision, the Foundation wants to know EVERYTHING about a subject, even if someone is lost, but as you've said, it seems over in many ways, including containment methods and experimentation. I'll resume the SCP's narration to only one host and how it reacts to the SCP and I will measure the reactions of the Foundation accordingly.

I'll edit the first post once more. Thank you again.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Readers & Reviewers

item.content:encoded:
I found this podcast not that long ago but the videos in the podcast have really good discussions in them and really good editing. Yes the podcast/channel IS new but it still bothers me so go take a look at it if you don't like it leave if ya like it sub and show the support this man needs
Here's the link to the channel:
https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC3-tiMJw4PmyfQR-PlrGvZg

item.title: Re: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers
item.content:encoded:
Greeting everyone my name is classified of course but PLEASE call me tukapee or tuk for short, overtime I may give out my real name, unless thats a requirement, anyway the best food to eat with friends are berries! Cause their juicy, healthy, and full of flavor! I hope I can be agreat member to the community and have my scp on the list with the othera^^


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Introductions
Forum thread: JANUARY 2021 INTROS - Writers

Exactly: living things get electrocuted (killed by electricity), and wardrobes aren't alive. (Yes, SCP-002 and similar "oddly living" entities exist; but nothing in this write-up implies that #4609 is one.)


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-4609

item.title: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded:
Yes, that helped. That's too bad about Koth, but if I do decide to change I guess I can just chuck some underscores in there or something.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: Changing wikidot username

item.title: Re: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)
item.content:encoded:
Heyo followup!

SAND

+ show block
– hide block

The concept:
I'm still not particularly convinced on the 'ballet dance fighting', to me it still just feels a bit unrelated? and it kind takes focus way from the narratives main theme. From what I've seen, nothing about the concept or narrative would really need to change if was removed.

The narrative:
As for the narrative, I'm still not really invested in the characters emotionally. They don't really have much of a progression, or any interesting traits. As it stands they feel like test dummies, they don't really have much of a conflict with he Foundation or any changing ideals. Which makes the narrative feel a bit flat, along with making it hard to get invested in the characters themselves.- what about each of them will make me invested? do their deaths affect the Foundation in any way? in what ways do they exhibit the traits shown?

Along with this, I've noticed the Foundation is bit overreactive with the skip and its hosts. it feels like every time the skip shows unfavourable behaviour, the host gets killed and the skip is moved along to the next person. And then by the end they go way overboard in protecting it?- it sounds more plausible (at least to me), for the host to be kept in containment and testing ceased, when they noticed the traits you mentioned start to manifest.

Red or green?
Due to my afore mentioned concerns, no-greenlight

If you would like me review this (or any concept) further, then please feel free to send me a PM or ask via IRC. Response times generally range from 2-12 hours over PMs. If your looking for additional critiques/perspectives in general, then I would highly recommend contacting the butterfly squad :)

Cheers,

RED


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: Sword Ballerina (Seeking Greenlight)

item.title: Re: (Seeking Greenlight) Just a normal old mirror.
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the critique Mr. Desert

Im really bad at writing these idea things, but ill be sure to use your critique when (probably) rewriting this idea!


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (Seeking Greenlight) Just a normal old mirror.

item.title: Re: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
They ask in #thecritters for people to give critique, and reach out to many people on the Butterfly Squad Roster. You don't need to get greenlights to publish an SCP, but its recommended you get critique and go through the process because it will help you as an author. Publication isn't strict, its how the viewer base votes that takes out many articles that don't follow the greenlight process.

Not just moderators and admins give greenlights either. Anyone who qualifies for an author page, on crit staff, completed a flight, or chose for the Reviewers Spotlight are capable of greenlighting.

I hope this was helpful.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?

item.title: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?
item.content:encoded:
How is it even possible that SCPs are actually even allowed for publication? I have only ever seen one green light go out throughout the tens of ideas I have over viewed.

Were the first couple thousand SCPs published before the publication became so strict?

I'm just not sure if people aren't creative enough, don't think it through enough, or if the moderators are too unwilling to give it a try.


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: How do SCP ideas even make it to two greenlights?

item.title: Re: (Seeking Greenlight) Just a normal old mirror.
item.content:encoded:
Hello MapleDraws! let's take a look :)

SAND

+ show block
– hide block

The concept:

Elevator Pitch: This SCP is essentially a mirror from an unknown company that, when people enter the reflection radius of it, it "sucks" them into a mirror dimension, where creepy stuff happens.

Overall, this is too vague to really get an idea of what the skip actually is. Currently, there isn't really anything expands on the 'unknown company', or the 'mirror dimension' or what this 'creepy stuff' is. Could you elaborate a bit more on the above examples? what parts do these elements play in the narrative? how will they make me as the reader invested?

Along with this, the mirror doesn't really seem to have a purpose? what exactly was it made to achieve? why will that be important? how will its purpose affect the narrative?

Currently the concept as a whole feels dim-of the dozen, largely due to it not separating itself enough from similar premises enough. Theres quite a few 'mirror dimensions' that have been portrayed in other media, and on the wiki before. What will give this concept an edge over similar ideas/premises?

The narrative

Central Narrative: It revolves around 3 expedition logs in which more about the world is developed, including wierd entities who hate outsiders and can do some kind of wierd "magic"

Again, this is still too vague to really get an idea of what the narrative will look like in the draft. There isn't really an arc present here?

Here is some stuff I would recommend elaborating or expanding on more:
how and where was the skip initially found? what actually takes place in these expedition logs? who are the characters? whats the payoff? what is the main conflict? and how should the narrative as a whole make me feel?

As it stands the narrative doesn't really grab my attention, as theres little more context than "mirror dimension was explored by the Foundation", and there doesn't seem to be any conflict (which makes the narrative as a whole feels bit flat). Nothing really works to answer the questions raised in the concept or further explore the native life there either.- why are they so hostile? what about them is "weird"?

Red or green?
Due to my afore mentioned concerns, no-greenlight.

If you would like me review this (or any concept) further, then please feel free to send me a PM or ask via IRC. Response times generally range from 2-12 hours over PMs. If your looking for additional critiques/perspectives in general, then I would highly recommend contacting the butterfly squad :)

Cheers,

RED


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: (Seeking Greenlight) Just a normal old mirror.

item.title: Re: Changing wikidot username
item.content:encoded:
How did you determine that Koth was taken?

I went to http://www.wikidot.com/user:info/koth


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Questions Desk
Forum thread: Changing wikidot username

item.title: Re: Favorite video game quotes?
item.content:encoded:
"The darkest pit of hell has opened to swallow you whole; don't keep the Devil waiting, friend."
-FNaF of all places

The world calls for the skills of us horrid men. And we answer. No greater good. No just cause. Just a paycheck, and another dead man.
-MGS V

Joshua Graham: "For many of us, the road is a difficult one, but the path is always there for us to follow, no matter how many times we may fall."
Courier: "Do you ever 'fall?'"
"Every day. Some days are… harder than others."
-Fallout: New Vegas


Forum category: General Discussion / Games and Hobbies
Forum thread: Favorite video game quotes?

item.title: Re: Deletions 70: If the fact will not fit the theory – let the theory go.
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5334 is a 8 feet long, 1 foot wide, and 2 feet, 6 inches tall Chilopoda. SCP-5334's body segments are covered in a thick shell, with dark brown stripes covering the black shell. Protruding from the shells are 2 feet tall horns, which are used as a defense mechanism. A pair of legs appear on every body segment. SCP-5334 has around 173 body segments. SCP-5334's head is an inch longer than its body segments, and contains a horn at the tip of its head. The horn is a inch larger than the previous horns. Two 1 foot antennae protrude from the face of SCP-5334 as well in back end. Two pincers, 1 foot and 8 inches each, are attached to SCP-5334's head, which are used to grab and crush prey.

Deleted at -15 and three votes.


Forum category: Site Announcements and Proposals / Announcements
Forum thread: Deletions 70: If the fact will not fit the theory – let the theory go.

item.title: I'm loosing my shit over the images
item.content:encoded:
God, these images are so goddamn cool. Like, they are so unique and just straight-up surreal it looks like something out of an actual art piece.

Aside from that though, it was a really good read. Loved it.

Wait, you did The Password and Tiamat? Two of my fav Series VIs, from the same person! I don't have much to say that hasn't been said, but good job.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5109

(Would give +5, one for each head)

Hell(s), this is good! I've found that a lot of SCP Articles that are really good also tend to be really long, which I don't mind, but it'd nice to have a lovely shorter article. It plays with the idea of Thoughtforms in a way that ties the article in well with the SCP Universe while also providing a very un-SCP story. (Branda/Tiamat OTP)
Now I'm gonna go check out more of your stuff.


Forum category: Per Page Discussions / Per page discussions
Forum thread: SCP-5866

item.title: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Psychological

Page Layout (Optional): Interview articles, "Sit-in Classes"

Elevator Pitch: Students who want college credit attend a class taught by an unusual professor.

Central Narrative: Letters from "South Euton University" are sent out to high school and college students who want college credit. The class being taught is labeled "Philosophy of Existence," and is taught by Professor Phillip Oliver Zofia. The classroom has an unusual setup, with strange posters and maps that have countries improperly labeled. Above the whiteboard is a large poster, upon which is written, "Cogito, Ergo Sum." The professor himself is a man described as "gaunt," "faint," even "ethereal."

+ show block
– hide block

The class begins with the professor providing the students with an initial quiz. The questions are very easy (2+2=?), but the students fail when they provide standard answers. The professor then proceeds to teach the class, telling them blatantly incorrect answers, and then testing them on the incorrect knowledge. Those that refuse to answer "properly" are given personal instruction by Dr. Zofia. The sessions apparently take half an hour, though one student describes it feeling as though she was in there for days (vague as to whether she was or not). Invariably, the students start giving him the answers he wants, whether through exasperation or actually starting to buy into his teaching. They become increasingly suggestible as this goes on. Simultaneously, Dr. Zofia grows stronger, fuller, more substantial.

The final test consists of a piece of paper, asking, "Cogito, ergo sum. Concur?" There is also a line where the students write their names, which they invariably do. At the conclusion of the test, the students disappear, while Dr. Zofia seems to become completely rejuvenated. The SCP agent monitoring this situation (implied that Zofia is amused by their presence and is toying with them) offers them a letter and an invitation to join them next semester, and then is ushered out.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Aside from the oddities of the classroom, the central question, "I think, therefore I am," is explored. If one stops thinking, do they really exist? And if one's thoughts supercede another's, does that not make them more you, than you? This is also meant to be mild commentary on the education system caring more about correct answers than understanding material.

Additional Notes:


Forum category: Writing Help / Help: Ideas Critique
Forum thread: SCP Idea: Cogito, Ergo Sum
